title: Repair fake onboarding closure and cut over real API usage

stories.title: Remove fake success from onboarding backend contract
stories.scope: Make the Device V2 onboarding backend honest. GET must return actions, retryableActions, and a summary from the latest real store run. PLAN must stop using a fixed placeholder list and validate server monitor mode only when required. ENSURE must stop writing hardcoded success, load a concrete config source, and merge results into the latest real store run instead of inventing a synthetic success path.
nonGoals: Do not invent remote success when the real business contract is not safely callable.
Do not widen scope into full SNMP trap support.
acceptance: GET onboarding returns actions, retryableActions, and a derived summary from the latest real store run.
PLAN onboarding is not a fixed placeholder response and rejects missing server monitor mode only when the target device actually requires it.
ENSURE onboarding no longer writes hardcoded success or dispatch_result=success placeholders.
ENSURE onboarding either loads a concrete config source or fails loudly with a concrete config-source error; it must not treat DefaultOnboardingConfig as a complete runtime source.
ENSURE onboarding merges action evidence into the latest real store run instead of creating a synthetic placeholder run.
notes: If a real contract is missing, prefer blocked evidence over hardcoded success.

stories.title: Cut DeviceV2ManagementRedesign over to onboarding APIs
stories.scope: Change the DeviceV2ManagementRedesign continue-onboarding UX so it uses the new onboarding plan/ensure/read APIs instead of startDeviceV2StorePipelineReq or retryDeviceV2StorePipelineReq. Keep the first phase single-device-only rule, require explicit server monitor mode selection when needed, and render onboarding action evidence directly instead of using store observations as a substitute.
nonGoals: Do not redesign unrelated Device V2 panels.
Do not keep the old store-start path behind the same continue-onboarding action.
acceptance: The continue-onboarding CTA in DeviceV2ManagementRedesign no longer calls startDeviceV2StorePipelineReq or retryDeviceV2StorePipelineReq.
The redesign view reads onboarding evidence from onboarding APIs instead of treating store run observations as onboarding action evidence.
The UI still enforces one-device-only execution.
When the backend requires server monitor mode, the UI collects an explicit choice before ensure.
The UI renders backend blocked/failed/unknown action evidence directly without masking it as generic success.
notes: Current bug anchor: DeviceV2ManagementRedesign.vue still routes continue-onboarding through the old store pipeline.
Keep the single-device rule visible to the user.

stories.title: Close validation and evidence gaps for the repaired batch
stories.scope: Finish this repair batch by running the intended verification and filling the missing durable artifacts. Add any missing D2ON evidence summaries that were previously marked done without files, run browser/devtools validation for the redesign page if the local target is available, and replace TBD placeholders in batch-001-summary, final-readiness, and review with concrete findings and residual risks.
nonGoals: Do not claim visual validation happened if the page or credentials are unavailable.
Do not mark the batch done while acceptance artifacts are still missing.
acceptance: Missing evidence summaries from prior done stories are created or their absence is explicitly explained with a blocker.
Browser/devtools validation is run for the redesign page, or a concrete blocker is documented with the attempted target.
docs/prd-autodev/device-v2-onboarding-observability/review.md is no longer TBD and clearly lists any remaining blockers.
notes: This story is documentation and evidence closure, but it is not optional.
If D2ON-006 or D2ON-007 are still materially incomplete, keep this story blocked.
